channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: When protests may justify missing work and when they don’t
item.description: In Kenya, protests often disrupt daily life and many workers cite them as reasons for missing work. However, legal experts from MMTK Law caution that simply saying 'there were protests' does not autom...

item.title: NASA Predicts China May Send Astronauts on Lunar Orbit Mission by 2027
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has expressed expectations that China could conduct a crewed mission around the Moon in 2027, a statement that intensifies perceptions of a renewed space race between...

item.title: Maryland Appellate Court Upholds Access to Correctional Facility Videos Under Public Information Law
item.description: The Maryland Court of Appeals recently affirmed a lower court ruling requiring the Department of Public Safety and Correctional Services to release video recordings related to the fatal stabbing of in...

item.title: CDF Munir reaffirms Pakistan’s commitment to fight terrorism and honours martyrs
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces and Chief of Army Staff, Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, emphasised that Pakistan’s fight against terrorism will go on with full national determination until lasting peace and s...

item.title: Farrah Fawcett's Dramatic Career Transformation in the 1980s
item.description: Farrah Fawcett, born Mary Ferrah Leni Fawcett, rose to fame as a star of the first season of 'Charlie’s Angels' but spent the 1980s redefining her career beyond her iconic 'pin-up' image. Initially, h...

item.title: Settle Easy, sixth-largest conveyancer in Australia, enters administration
item.description: Settle Easy, recognised as Australia's sixth-largest conveyancing firm, has entered administration under SALEA, signalling significant financial difficulties for the company. The firm, which has longs...

item.title: New Coalitions Bill to Guide Municipal Governance Ahead of November Elections
item.description: The South African government is set to implement the Municipal Structures Amendment Bill, commonly referred to as the 'coalitions bill', ahead of the local government elections on 4 November 2026. Co-...

item.title: Analysis: Labor faces strong backlash over budget measures while One Nation gains momentum
item.description: The Albanese Labor government is dealing with significant criticism following its latest federal budget, which was promoted as focusing on intergenerational equity and housing affordability. However, ...

item.title: Registrar reviews Chadema and CCM statements over potential legal breaches
item.description: The Office of the Registrar of Political Parties has requested Chadema to explain itself following allegations of violating the Political Parties Act. The allegations were connected to statements made...

item.title: Mexico works fast to finish $500 million airport upgrade ahead of 2026 World Cup
item.description: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup waka closer, Mexican authorities dey hustle to complete big renovation work for Benito Juárez International Airport, the busiest airport for the country. The $500 million pr...

item.title: Gender-responsive procurement boosting women-owned businesses in Kenya
item.description: Women entrepreneurs in Kenya face significant hurdles in accessing government and private sector contracts due to cultural biases, lack of capital, and structural barriers, despite the 30 per cent rul...

item.title: President Samia Discusses Development and Trade Cooperation with Chinese Delegation
item.description: On May 20, 2026, President Samia Suluhu hosted a senior delegation from the Chinese Communist Party (CCP), led by Liu Haixing, at the State House in Dar es Salaam. The discussions focused on strengthe...

item.title: PMD Warns of Severe Heatwave Across Pakistan During Eid Holidays
item.description: According to the Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD), heatwave conditions are likely to persist throughout Pakistan from May 25 to May 31, coinciding with Eidul Azha celebrations on May 27. The f...

item.title: Centre opposes new hydroelectric projects in Uttarakhand’s upper Ganga basins, allows only seven ongoing projects
item.description: The Union government informed the Supreme Court on Wednesday that it does not support the sanctioning of any new hydroelectric projects in the Alaknanda and Bhagirathi river basins in Uttarakhand, whi...

item.title: Migrant Workers in Aramco’s Supply Chain Face Unsafe Conditions and Lack of Compensation
item.description: A recent report by the human rights group FairSquare has highlighted serious labour rights violations affecting migrant workers employed through contractors and subcontractors in Saudi Aramco’s supply...

item.title: FIFA World Cup Expansion Raises Logistical and Competitive Concerns
item.description: FIFA’s decision to expand the World Cup to include more teams and host cities has sparked debate over whether the tournament has grown too large to manage effectively. While the expansion aims to incr...

item.title: Trump Supporter Uses Fasting as a Strategy to Save Money Amid Financial Struggles
item.description: During a recent segment on CNN, anchor Erin Burnett expressed her shock after hearing a Trump supporter from Georgia describe how he and his wife manage their finances amid economic challenges worsene...

item.title: Improving Bankability is Key to Unlocking South Africa’s Infrastructure Investment
item.description: South Africa’s infrastructure ambitions face a critical bottleneck—not a shortage of capital, but a lack of bankable projects. According to recent discussions at the South Africa Infrastructure Invest...

item.title: Court-sacked Lawmaker Moves to APC After PDP Crisis
item.description: Honourable Dachung Musa Bagos, wey dey represent Jos South/Jos East Federal Constituency for Plateau State, don explain why e comot from PDP go join APC. According to am, na political survival make am...

item.title: Cape Town Greenlights Mixed-Use Development with Affordable Housing in CBD
item.description: The City of Cape Town has approved a plan to redevelop a municipal parking lot next to the Civic Centre into a mixed-use development that will include affordable housing. The project forms part of a b...
